def: An ongoing fee deducted from mutual fund assets to cover marketing, distribution, and shareholder service costs. This fee is baked into the fund expense ratio and reduces investor returns annually.
avoid: Choose no-load funds with zero 12b-1 fees, or buy index funds directly from providers, and review fund prospectuses before investing.

def: A one-time charge levied when opening a new account, service, or line of credit. Common with cell phone plans, credit cards, and utilities.
avoid: Ask providers to waive activation fees during sign-up promotions or loyalty programs; negotiate for a credit on your first bill.

def: A broad fee covering general overhead costs such as billing, record-keeping, and customer support. Often added to insurance policies, loans, and service contracts without itemization.
avoid: Request a detailed breakdown of administrative costs and negotiate to have them capped or waived; compare providers with lower admin fee structures.

def: The gradual reduction of a loan balance through scheduled payments over time. While not a direct fee, amortization schedules can hide how much interest you pay upfront compared to principal.
cost: Varies by loan term and rate
avoid: Use amortization calculators to see total interest costs; make extra principal payments early to reduce total interest paid over the life of the loan.

def: Costs associated with resolving disputes through binding arbitration rather than court. Many contracts require arbitration and split the filing and hearing costs between parties.
avoid: Review contracts for mandatory arbitration clauses before signing; negotiate for fee-shifting provisions or class-action waivers.

def: A sales charge or commission paid when selling certain mutual fund shares, typically within a specified holding period. The fee decreases over time and may disappear after several years.
avoid: Choose no-load mutual funds or ETFs; hold shares until the back-end load expires; review share class structures before purchasing.

def: A legal relationship where one party temporarily holds another party property, often incurring storage or handling fees. Bailment fees appear in moving, storage, and repair contracts.
avoid: Read storage and repair contracts before signing; ask about daily or monthly bailment charges; remove property promptly after work is completed.

def: A charge for moving debt from one credit card to another, typically a percentage of the transferred amount. Often associated with balance transfer promotions.
avoid: Use cards offering 0% balance transfer fees; calculate whether the transfer fee outweighs interest savings; limit transfers to promotional periods with waived fees.

def: A commission charged by a broker for executing trades on your behalf, including buying or selling stocks, bonds, or other securities.
avoid: Use commission-free brokers like Robinhood, Fidelity, or Schwab; trade in larger blocks to minimize per-trade fees.

def: A penalty charged when canceling a contract, reservation, or service before the agreed term ends. Common with hotels, flights, gyms, and subscription services.
avoid: Read cancellation policies before booking; choose refundable rates or free-cancellation options; cancel within the grace period if the contract allows.

def: A charge for withdrawing cash against your credit card limit, often with a higher interest rate and no grace period. Interest accrues immediately.
avoid: Never use credit cards for cash advances; use a debit card or personal loan instead.

def: An administrative charge assessed when modifying the scope of work in a construction, renovation, or service contract after the agreement has been signed.
avoid: Get all change orders in writing with pricing before work begins; bundle multiple changes into a single order to reduce per-change costs.

def: A fee added to cover costs of meeting regulatory or legal compliance requirements, often passed to consumers as a line item.
avoid: Ask providers to explain what specific regulations the fee covers; compare providers with lower compliance charges.

def: A charge for the privilege of paying a bill by credit card, online, or over the phone rather than by cash or check.
avoid: Pay by ACH transfer or paper check to avoid convenience fees; ask if the fee can be waived.

def: An optional insurance product sold alongside loans that pays the loan balance if the borrower dies, becomes disabled, or loses their job. Often high-cost with limited benefit.
industry: Auto Loans, Personal Loans, Credit Cards
avoid: Decline all credit insurance products; buy term life or disability insurance separately at better rates.

def: A charge for exceeding the monthly data cap on a mobile phone or internet plan. These fees can escalate quickly with streaming or large downloads.
avoid: Monitor data usage through provider apps; set data alerts and caps on your device; switch to an unlimited plan if you consistently exceed your limit.

def: A markup added by car dealers for preparing a vehicle for sale, including cleaning, inspection, and paperwork. Largely a profit center with no real cost basis.
avoid: Challenge dealer prep fees as pure profit; negotiate the total price of the car and refuse to pay separate prep fees.

def: A charge for transporting goods or services to your location, commonly added to furniture, appliance, online orders, and large-item purchases.
avoid: Look for free delivery promotions; bundle multiple items into one delivery to reduce per-item costs.

def: A fixed charge for evaluating a problem, common with auto repair, HVAC, plumbing, and electronics services. Often applied even if you decline the repair.
avoid: Ask if the diagnostic fee is waived if you approve the repair; shop around for free diagnostic offers.

def: A fee for removing and discarding old items, equipment, or materials after a service or installation.
avoid: Dispose of old items yourself at a recycling center to avoid disposal fees.

def: A fee charged by dealerships and lenders for processing paperwork related to a sale or loan, including title work, registration, and filing. This is a profit center.
avoid: Negotiate documentation fees down or ask for them to be removed entirely; compare total out-the-door prices including doc fees across dealerships.

def: A penalty for ending a contract, lease, or service agreement before the full term expires. Common in cell phone plans, leases, and subscription services.
avoid: Choose month-to-month or no-contract plans; read the early termination policy before signing; ask for a waiver if you have a valid hardship.

def: A premium charge for after-hours, weekend, or urgent service calls from contractors, plumbers, electricians, or locksmiths.
avoid: Plan ahead for routine maintenance to avoid emergencies; ask about emergency service rates before calling.

def: A recurring charge for leasing equipment such as modems, routers, cable boxes, or medical devices instead of buying them outright.
avoid: Buy your own compatible equipment to eliminate rental fees; return equipment promptly when canceling service.

def: A contract provision allowing the price or fee to increase under certain conditions, such as rising material costs, inflation, or scope changes.
avoid: Request a fixed-price contract with no escalation clauses; negotiate caps on escalation percentages.

def: A charge added by hospitals, clinics, or medical centers to cover building maintenance, equipment, and operational overhead. Often billed separately from the professional services.
avoid: Ask if you can receive care at an outpatient clinic rather than a hospital facility to reduce facility fees.

def: A charge for purchases made in a foreign currency or processed through an international bank. Applies to credit and debit card transactions abroad.
avoid: Use credit cards that charge no foreign transaction fees; pay in the local currency rather than dynamic currency conversion.

def: A sales commission charged when purchasing certain mutual fund shares, deducted immediately from the initial investment amount.
avoid: Choose no-load mutual funds or ETFs; work with fee-only financial advisors who do not charge commissions.

def: Guaranteed Asset Protection insurance covers the difference between a car actual cash value and the remaining loan balance if the vehicle is totaled or stolen.
avoid: Get GAP coverage from your auto insurer for $20–$40/year instead of the dealership.

def: A charge assessed on accounts with no transactions for a defined period, common with prepaid cards, gift cards, and dormant bank accounts.
cost: $2–$15 per month after 6–12 months of inactivity
avoid: Make a small transaction every few months to keep accounts active; close accounts you no longer use.

def: A contractual obligation where one party agrees to cover losses, damages, or legal costs incurred by the other party. Can expose you to significant unlabeled costs.
avoid: Negotiate caps on indemnification liability; exclude coverage for the other party negligence; have a lawyer review indemnity clauses.

def: Any unnecessary, excessive, or deceptive fee added to a bill or contract with little or no legitimate service provided. Junk fees are pure profit for the business.
avoid: Read every line item on bills; ask bluntly: What is this fee for and can you remove it? Use AI bill analysis to detect junk fees.

def: A penalty charged when a payment is not received by the due date. May trigger a higher penalty interest rate.
avoid: Set up automatic payments; request a one-time courtesy waiver if you have a good payment history.

def: A predetermined amount specified in a contract that one party must pay the other if they breach the agreement.
avoid: Negotiate lower liquidated damages amounts; include cure periods before damages apply.

def: A deceptive practice where lenders add unnecessary products into a loan without the borrower explicit consent.
industry: Auto Loans, Personal Loans, Payday Lending
avoid: Read all loan documents carefully before signing; decline all optional add-ons and products.

def: A recurring charge for the upkeep of equipment, property, software, or accounts. Common with bank accounts, HOA dues, and equipment leases.
avoid: Choose no-maintenance-fee bank accounts; negotiate maintenance fees in service contracts.

def: An additional charge by car dealerships above MSRP due to high demand, limited supply, or vehicle popularity. Pure dealer profit.
avoid: Refuse to pay any market adjustment markup; expand your search radius to dealers in less competitive markets.

def: The percentage added to the cost of materials by contractors, builders, or service providers. Often 10%–50%.
avoid: Request that materials be billed at cost with a disclosed markup; supply your own materials where feasible.

def: A recurring monthly charge for maintaining an account, service, or membership.
avoid: Choose no-monthly-fee accounts; review all subscriptions biennially and cancel unused services.

def: A charge when a transaction is declined due to insufficient funds in your account.
avoid: Sign up for overdraft protection or link a savings account; monitor account balances closely.

def: An upfront charge by a lender for processing, underwriting, and funding a loan. Typically a percentage of the loan amount.
avoid: Compare APRs including origination fees across lenders; ask for lender credits.

def: A charge incurred when you spend more than you have in your checking account and the bank covers the shortfall.
avoid: Opt out of overdraft coverage so transactions are declined without a fee; link a savings account.

def: A monthly charge for receiving a physical paper statement by mail instead of electronic statements.
avoid: Switch to electronic statements and online account access to eliminate this fee.

def: A charge for required building, construction, or renovation permits from local government authorities.
avoid: Verify that your contractor actually obtains permits; ask to see the permit documentation.

def: An extra charge for upgraded services, faster processing, or enhanced features.
avoid: Evaluate whether the premium service is worth the extra cost; ask for premium fees to be waived as a loyalty perk.

def: A catch-all charge for administrative handling of payments, applications, claims, or transactions. Often appears without specific justification.
avoid: Ask what specific processing activity the fee covers; negotiate for it to be included in the base price.

def: A line-item charge on bills to recoup costs of complying with government regulations, licensing, or regulatory fees.
avoid: Ask for a detailed explanation of what regulations are being recovered; compare fees across providers.

def: The increased premium charged when an insurance policy or service contract renews, often higher than the initial rate.
avoid: Shop around before renewing; negotiate renewal rates with your current provider using competitor quotes.

def: A mandatory daily charge added by hotels for amenities like pool access, gym use, Wi-Fi, and newspapers. Not included in the advertised room rate.
avoid: Check for resort fees before booking; call the hotel directly and ask for the total price including all fees.

def: A charge when a payment fails due to insufficient funds, closed account, or stop payment.
avoid: Keep sufficient funds in your payment account; set up low-balance alerts.

def: A general fee added to bills for providing a service, often without specifying what service is being compensated.
avoid: Ask what the service charge covers; negotiate service charges in business contracts.

def: A one-time charge for establishing a new account, line of credit, service subscription, or system installation.
avoid: Ask for setup fee waivers during promotional periods; choose providers promoting no-setup-fee sign-ups.

def: A periodic charge for receiving account statements, usually applied to paper statements.
avoid: Opt for electronic statements exclusively to eliminate this fee.

def: A charge for requesting your bank to stop processing a check or recurring payment before it is cashed.
avoid: Use online bill pay instead of writing checks to avoid needing stop payments.

def: An additional charge added to the standard price for a product or service, often due to external factors.
avoid: Ask for all-in pricing before committing; compare total costs including surcharges across providers.

def: A penalty for ending an agreement prematurely, covering any end-of-contract charges beyond early termination.
avoid: Read termination clauses carefully before signing; negotiate a reduced or waived termination fee.

def: A charge for moving to a higher service tier, account level, or membership category.
avoid: Ask about upgrade fees before initiating tier changes; negotiate for the fee to be waived.

def: A per-transaction charge for processing a payment, trade, or account activity.
avoid: Minimize transactions by batching; choose accounts with unlimited free transactions.

def: A charge for moving funds, assets, or accounts from one institution to another.
avoid: Use no-fee transfer services like Zelle or ACH; ask your institution to waive transfer fees.

def: A flat fee charged by service providers for traveling to your location, separate from labor or parts.
avoid: Ask if the trip charge is credited toward the total bill if you approve repairs.

def: A charge for applying rust-preventative coating to the underside of a vehicle, often sold at highly inflated prices by dealerships.
avoid: Decline dealer-offered undercoating; most modern cars come factory-protected.

def: A fraudulent practice where a healthcare provider bills for a more expensive service than what was actually performed.
avoid: Always review your Explanation of Benefits against what was actually done; verify procedure codes.

def: A tactic where a business intentionally provides unclear pricing information, hiding fees until the final bill.
avoid: Always ask for total all-in pricing upfront in writing; get detailed itemized quotes.

def: A charge for etching the VIN into car windows as theft deterrent, costing dealers very little but sold at high prices.
avoid: Decline VIN etching outright; buy a DIY kit online for under $20 if desired.

def: A charge for waiving something such as a cancellation penalty, a fee, or a contract requirement.
avoid: Ask for fee waivers as a courtesy before signing; negotiate the fee down or away.

def: A charge for sending or receiving money via wire transfer, domestic or international. Wires are faster but cost more than ACH.
avoid: Use ACH, Zelle, or Wise for lower-cost transfers instead of wire transfers.

def: A yearly charge imposed for the privilege of holding a credit card, membership, or account. Often found on rewards cards or premium accounts.
avoid: Choose no-annual-fee cards; call before the fee posts to ask for a retention offer or downgrade to a fee-free version.

def: A non-refundable fee charged to process an application for credit, rental housing, or services. Covers credit checks and administrative processing.
avoid: Ask if the fee can be waived or refunded upon approval; shop around for lenders or landlords with lower or no application fees.

def: A fee charged by HOAs, condo associations, or property managers for common-area maintenance, repairs, or reserves.
avoid: Review HOA documents before purchase for special assessment history; budget for potential assessments.

def: A charge for using an out-of-network automated teller machine, split between the ATM operator and your own bank.
avoid: Use in-network ATMs only; get cash back at retailers; switch to a bank that reimburses ATM fees.

def: A charge that triggers when a subscription or service contract renews automatically, often at a higher rate than the initial term.
avoid: Set calendar reminders before renewal dates; cancel auto-renewal immediately after sign-up.

def: A service charge added to hotel reservations, flight bookings, or event tickets by online travel agencies or third-party platforms.
avoid: Book directly with hotels or airlines to avoid third-party booking fees; compare total prices including fees.

def: Fees paid when finalizing a real estate transaction, including loan origination, appraisal, title insurance, and recording fees.
avoid: Shop for lenders with lower origination fees; negotiate for seller concessions; review the Loan Estimate carefully.

def: A fee charged by a lender to keep a line of credit or loan commitment open for a specified period.
avoid: Negotiate commitment fees upfront; close on the loan quickly to minimize the fee period.

def: A charge for sending a technician or service vehicle to your location, added to service calls for repairs or emergency work.
avoid: Ask if the dispatch fee is included in the service call; bundle multiple repairs into one visit.

def: A one-time charge to join a program, service, or membership. Found in gyms, insurance plans, and loyalty programs.
avoid: Ask for enrollment fee waivers during promotional periods; look for no-enrollment-fee programs.
